description: Explore Codex client capability modeling and init summary behavior for supported vs installed states
focus: resolver.ts; init capability summary; follow-up messaging; i18n
findings: `detectClientSupports()` models Codex CLI as supported/detected when `~/.codex` exists or `clientPaths.codexCLI` is set, but its capability map is fixed to `bootstrap=true,mcp=true,hook=false,skill=false`; this means init always renders Codex as `Skill=manual` and `Follow-up=manual step required`, regardless of installed MCP config (`packages/cli/src/config/resolver.ts`, `packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts`). `formatInitReasonMessage()` similarly switches to the manual-body unless any detected client has `skill=true`, so Codex-only setups get manual follow-up messaging. i18n already carries both summary headers/statuses in `en` and `zh-CN`, and regression coverage exists in `init-mcp-scope.test.ts` for interactive Codex output plus `config-install.test.ts` for TOML MCP install.

description: Explore where Codex init assets should be installed without conflicting with existing uncommitted template work
focus: init.ts; repo skill path; codex templates; non-destructive install behavior
findings: `initFabric` writes base assets to `.fabric/bootstrap/README.md`, `.fabric/agents.meta.json`, `.fabric/human-lock.json`, `.fabric/forensic.json`, plus Claude-only assets under `.claude/**` (`packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts:292-325`). Non-destructive behavior is mixed: core `.fabric/*` paths abort if they already exist unless `--force`, but Claude skill/hook/settings are skipped/merged when present (`init.ts:445-501`). Template resolution prefers repo-root `templates/**` from `process.cwd()` before package-local copies (`init.ts:411-443`, `bootstrap-guide.ts:63-95`), so current uncommitted edits in `templates/bootstrap/codex-AGENTS-header.md` and `templates/claude-skills/agents-md-init/SKILL.md` would affect `fab init`. `codex-AGENTS-header.md` appears present but not wired into bootstrap install yet; Codex bootstrap currently points to `.fabric/bootstrap/README.md` like other clients (`config/resolver.ts:193-203`, `bootstrap.ts:125-133`).

description: Explore existing init test patterns and identify the minimal regression suite for Codex phase-1 implementation
focus: init install tests; capability summary tests; non-destructive behavior; fixture strategy
findings: Current minimal regression suite can stay small and reuse existing fixture helpers in packages/cli/__tests__/helpers/init-test-utils.ts. Evidence: install behavior is covered by init-claude-install.test.ts (writes skill/hook/settings/bootstrap and hook gating); capability summary for Codex is already asserted in init-mcp-scope.test.ts via initCommand.run with TTY+console mocks; non-destructive semantics are isolated in init-nondestructive.test.ts and the regression case in init-force.test.ts. Safest phase-1 additions are to extend those files rather than add new harnesses.
